title: Debt payoff
description: Practice understanding amortization schedules and debt payoff timelines.

assessment_items.question_content:
Your credit card statement shows the following:

Statement| 
- | -:
New balance | $\$2{,}471.20$ 
Minimum payment due | $\$40.00$
Due date | June 8
**Interest calculation** |
APR on purchases | $19.24\%$

You have decided to pay $\$150$ each month on this credit card, and will not make any new purchases.

**How long will it take to pay off this card?**


[[☃ numeric-input 1]] months
hints: To calculate how many months it will take to pay off this credit card, use the Time-to-Pay-Off calculator.

Click on $\blue{\text{Show Financial Calculators}}$ box below the question. Fill in the values from the scenario.

- **Loan amount** is the **New Balance**.
- **Interest rate** is the **APR on purchases**.
- **Monthly payment** is **the amount you are paying**, not the minimum amount.

Enter the values as follows:

- **Loan amount**: $\$2471.20$
- **Interest rate**: $19.24\%$
- **Monthly payment**: $\$150$

The calculator will display the Time to Pay Off (Months) at the bottom.
It will take $\boxed{19}$ months to pay off the credit card.

assessment_items.question_content:
Your car loan statement shows the following:

Statement| 
- | -:
Balance | $\$18{,}550.50$ 
Payment amount | $\$400.00$
Due date | April 8
**Interest calculation** |
Interest rate | $15.5\%$

You have decided to pay a bit extra each month and are making $\$450$ payments, instead.

**How long will it take you to pay off this car?**


[[☃ numeric-input 1]] months
hints: To calculate how many months it will take to pay off this loan, use the Time-to-Pay-Off calculator.

Click on $\blue{\text{Show Financial Calculators}}$ box below the question. Fill in the values from the scenario.

- **Loan amount** is the **loan balance**.
- **Interest rate** is the **interest on the car loan**.
- **Monthly payment** is **the (higher) payment you are making ** every month

Enter the values as follows:

- **Loan amount**: $\$18{,}550{.}50$
- **Interest rate**: $15{.}5\%$
- **Monthly payment**: $\$450$

The calculator will display the Time to Pay Off (Months) at the bottom.
It will take $\boxed{59}$ months to pay off the car loan.

assessment_items.question_content:
Your mortgage statement shows the following:

Statement| 
- | -:
Current balance | $\$189{,}762.75$ 
Payment due | $\$1{,}600.00$
Due date | June 29
**Interest calculation** |
Interest rate | $8{.}5\%$

You have decided to start paying a little bit extra, and are making $\$1{,}675$ payment each month.

**How long will it take to pay off this mortgage?**


[[☃ numeric-input 1]] months
hints: To calculate how many months it will take to pay off this mortgage, use the Time-to-Pay-Off calculator.

Click on $\blue{\text{Show Financial Calculators}}$ box below the question. Fill in the values from the scenario.

- **Loan amount** is the **current mortgage balance**.
- **Interest rate** is the **interest rate on the mortgage**.
- **Monthly payment** is **the amount you are paying** every month

Enter the values as follows:

- **Loan amount**: $\$189{,}762{.}75$
- **Interest rate**: $8{.}5\%$
- **Monthly payment**: $\$1{,}675.00$

The calculator will display the Time to Pay Off (Months) at the bottom.
It will take $\boxed{230}$ months to pay off the mortgage.

assessment_items.question_content:
You are shopping for a new car that costs $\$25{,}000$ and find two dealerships with different monthly payment options:

1. Dealership A offers a monthly payment of $\$500$.
2. Dealership B offers a monthly payment of $\$400$.

**What is most likely the main reason for the difference in these payments?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

You and your friend Jordan are both shopping online for a new laptop priced at $\$1{,}200$. You each apply for "Buy Now, Pay Later" option over $12$ months. However, you notice different monthly payment offers:

1. You see a monthly payment of $\$105$.
2. Jordan sees a monthly payment of $\$120$.

**What is most likely the main reason for the difference in these payments?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Your car loan has a variable interest rate. The rate changed from $4{.}99\%$ to $6{.}99\%$.

**What effect will this change have?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It will take longer to pay off the loan.
It will lower your credit score.

assessment_items.question_content:
Emma and Max each take out a loan of $\$20{,}000$ to be repaid over $5$ years with fixed monthly payments. Because of her excellent credit rating, Emma gets a loan with an annual percentage rate (APR) of $4\%$. Max, who has a fair credit rating, gets a loan with an APR of $7\%$.

| Emma | Max  
- | :-: | :-:
Principal |  $\$20{,}000$ |  $\$20{,}000$
Term | $5$ years  | $5$ years 
Credit rating | Excellent | Fair
APR | $4\%$ | $7\%$


**Complete the following sentence by selecting the correct options from the dropdowns:**

Compared to Emma, Max will have [[☃ dropdown 1]] monthly payments and will pay [[☃ dropdown 2]] total interest.
hints: Think about how the annual percentage rate (APR) affects both the monthly payments and the total interest paid when the loan amount and repayment term are the same.
Emma's APR is $4\%$, while Max's APR is $7\%$. Since Max has a higher APR, think how a *higher* APR impacts the cost of borrowing.
A *higher* APR means that the borrower is charged more interest on the loan. This results in *higher* monthly payments and a *greater* amount of total interest paid over the life of the loan.
Compared to Emma, Max will have $\boxed{\text{higher}}$ monthly payments and will pay $\boxed{\text{more}}$ total interest over the life of the loan.

assessment_items.question_content:
You are considering two loan options for borrowing $\$20{,}000$ to finance a new car. Both loans have the same annual percentage rate (APR) of $6\%$, but the loan terms are different. Option A has a loan term of **2 years**, and Option B has a loan term of **3 years**.

| Option A | Option B  
- | :-: | :-:
Principal |  $\$20{,}000$ |  $\$20{,}000$
Term | $2$ years  | $3$ years 
APR | $6\%$ | $6\%$


**Complete the following sentence by selecting the correct options from the dropdowns:**

With Option B ($3$**-year term**), your monthly payments will be [[☃ dropdown 1]] compared to Option A ($2$**-year term**). 

Over the life of the loan, you will pay [[☃ dropdown 2]] total interest with Option B compared to Option A.
hints: Think about how the loan term affects both the monthly payment and the total interest paid when the loan amount and interest rate are the same.
Option A term is $2$ years, while Option B is $3$ years. Since Option B has a longer term, think how a *longer* term impacts the cost of borrowing.
A longer loan term means lower monthly payments, but you'll pay *more* in total interest because it gives interest more time to accumulate.

With Option B ($3$**-year term**), your monthly payments will be $\boxed{\text{lower}}$ compared to Option A ($2$**-year term**).

Over the life of the loan, you will pay $\boxed{\text{more}}$ total interest with Option B compared to Option A.

assessment_items.question_content:
You are deciding between two loan amounts to fund your education. Both loans have the same annual percentage rate (APR) of $5\%$ and the same loan term of $10$ years.

| Option A | Option B  
- | :-: | :-:
Principal |  $\$20{,}000$ |  $\$30{,}000$
Term | $10$ years  | $10$ years 
APR | $5\%$ | $5\%$


**Complete the following sentence by selecting the correct options from the dropdowns:**

With Option B ($\$30{,}000$ loan), your monthly payments will be [[☃ dropdown 1]] compared to Option A ($\$20{,}000$ loan). 

Over the life of the loan, you will pay [[☃ dropdown 2]] total interest with Option B compared to Option A.
hints: Think about how the loan amount affects both the monthly payment and the total interest paid when the loan term and interest rate are the same.
The Option A loan amount is $\$20{,}000$ while Option B loan amount is $\$30{,}000$. Since Option B has a higher loan amount think how a *higher* amount impacts the cost of borrowing.

A *higher* loan amount generally leads to larger monthly payments because the amount that needs to be repaid is greater. This also increases the total amount of interest paid over the life of the loan, as interest is typically calculated based on the balance of the loan.

With Option B ($\$30{,}000$**-loan amount**), your monthly payments will be $\boxed{\text{higher}}$ compared to Option A ($\$20{,}000$**-loan amount**).

Over the life of the loan, you will pay $\boxed{\text{more}}$ total interest with Option B compared to Option A.

choices.content: They have the same credit scores.

assessment_items.question_content:
Bob and Nancy need $\$25{,}000$ to buy a car. They both go to the credit union to apply for a $5$-year car loan. The credit union shows them two different amortization schedules:

|| **Bob's loan** ||
Month | Payment amount | Interest | Principal paid | Remaining balance
- | - | - | - | -
$0$ | --- | --- | --- | $\$25{,}000.00$
$1$ | $\$506.91$ | $\$166.67$ | $\$340.24$ | $\$24{,}659.76$
$2$ | $\$506.91$ | $\$164.40$ | $\$342.51$ | $\$24{,}317.25$
$3$ | $\$506.91$ | $\$162.11$ | $\$344.80$ | $\$23{,}972.45$
... | ... | ...

|| **Nancy's loan** ||
Month | Payment amount | Interest | Principal paid | Remaining balance
- | - | - | - | -
$0$ | --- | --- | --- | $\$25{,}000.00$
$1$ | $\$471.78$ | $\$104.17$ | $\$367.61$ | $\$24{,}632.39$
$2$ | $\$471.78$ | $\$102.63$ | $\$369.15$ | $\$24{,}263.24$
$3$ | $\$471.78$ | $\$101.10$ | $\$370.68$ | $\$23{,}892.56$
... | ... | ... |

**Based on the amortization tables above, who has the lower credit score?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: they have the same credit scores.

assessment_items.question_content:
Carol and Mia need $\$10{,}000$ to start a business. They both go to the bank to get a personal loan. The bank shows them two different amortization schedules:

|| **Carol's loan** ||
Month | Payment amount | Interest | Principal paid | Remaining balance
- | - | - | - | -
$0$ | --- | --- | --- | $\$10{,}000.00$
$1$ | $\$318.00$ | $\$75.00$ | $\$243.00$ | $\$9{,}757.18$
$2$ | $\$318.00$ | $\$73.18$ | $\$244.82$ | $\$9{,}512.18$
$3$ | $\$318.00$ | $\$71.34$ | $\$246.66$ | $\$9{,}265.52$
... | ... | ...

|| **Mia's loan** ||
Month | Payment amount | Interest | Principal paid | Remaining balance
- | - | - | - | -
$0$ | --- | --- | --- | $\$10{,}000.00$
$1$ | $\$308.77$ | $\$58.33$ | $\$250.44$ | $\$9{,}749.56$
$2$ | $\$308.77$ | $\$56.87$ | $\$251.90$ | $\$9{,}497.66$
$3$ | $\$308.77$ | $\$55.40$ | $\$253.37$ | $\$9{,}244.29$
... | ... | ... |

**Based on the amortization tables above, who has the higher credit score?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They have the same credit scores.
